q1.question: How quickly will I get a response?
q1.answer: We aim to respond within 1 business day. For enterprise or sales inquiries we prioritise faster turnaround.

q2.question: Can you give a custom demo?
q2.answer: Certainly. We'd be happy to give a personal demo using your own website as an example. Schedule a 30-minute call via the form or the 'Schedule a call' button.

q3.question: Do you offer training or consultancy?
q3.answer: We can provide guided onboarding and platform walkthroughs. For ongoing consultancy or custom accessibility audits, contact us to discuss your needs.

q4.question: How does enterprise support work?
q4.answer: Enterprise gets dedicated support, SSO, and custom branding. Contact us for details.

q5.question: Do I need to book a demo?
q5.answer: No! You can start scanning immediately after signup. Just register and begin - no meetings required.

finalCta.title: Start scanning your website for free
finalCta.subtitle: Free forever for 1 website and up to 100 pages. No credit card required. Get your WCAG 2.2 report in minutes. Upgrade when you need more websites, pages, or advanced features.

q1.question: Can you guarantee legal compliance?
q1.answer: No tool can guarantee 100% legal compliance in all contexts. VexNexa detects and reports issues, assists remediation, and helps you adhere to WCAG and related standards. For legal risk assessment, consider an expert audit and ongoing governance process.

q2.question: How do page limits work?
q2.answer: Each plan includes a monthly page scan limit. A 'page' is one URL scanned. If you scan the same page multiple times in a month, each scan counts toward your limit. When you approach your limit, you'll receive a notification.

q3.question: What if I exceed my plan?
q3.answer: Free Plan: Service is blocked at limit. Upgrade to a paid plan to continue scanning. Paid plans: Service continues but you'll receive notifications to upgrade if you consistently exceed your limits.

q4.question: Do you support teams/SSO?
q4.answer: Pro plans support up to 5 team members. Business plans support up to 15 members with role-based access. SSO is available on Business and Enterprise plans. Contact us for Enterprise features.

q5.question: Can I export reports?
q5.answer: PDF export is available on all paid plans. Word export is available on Pro and Business plans. All exports include your scan results, prioritized issues, code snippets, and remediation recommendations.

q6.question: What's monitored continuously?
q6.answer: Continuous monitoring allows you to schedule automatic scans (daily, weekly, or monthly). You'll receive email alerts when scores drop or new issues are detected. This feature is available on Pro and Business plans.

q7.question: Do I need to schedule a demo?
q7.answer: No! VexNexa is 100% self-serve. Sign up, enter your URL, and get your accessibility report in minutes. We don't offer demos or sales calls.

q8.question: Is VexNexa an accessibility overlay?
q8.answer: No. We scan your code structure and give you WCAG violations to fix properly. We never inject widgets or scripts into your site. Overlays are controversial in the accessibility community—we analyze your actual code instead.

q9.question: Can VexNexa automatically fix issues?
q9.answer: No. Real accessibility requires proper code changes. We show you exactly what's wrong and how to fix it, but you need to make the changes yourself. Automated 'fixes' don't address root causes.

q10.question: Is there really a free plan?
q10.answer: Yes! Our free plan is forever free for 1 website and up to 100 pages per month. No credit card required. You get full WCAG 2.2 scanning, PDF reports, and 1 month of scan history. Upgrade only when you need more websites, pages, or advanced features like monitoring and Word exports.

q1.question: Can VexNexa guarantee legal compliance?
q1.answer: No tool can guarantee 100% legal compliance. VexNexa detects and reports accessibility issues, helps prioritize fixes, and supports WCAG and EAA readiness. Learn more about the [European Accessibility Act and what it means for agencies](/blog/what-is-the-european-accessibility-act). For legal risk assessment, consider combining automated scanning with expert review.

q2.question: Do I need an account to scan?
q2.answer: Yes, a free account is required. This lets us save your results, provide exports, and track improvements over time. No credit card needed to get started.

q3.question: Can I export branded reports for clients?
q3.answer: Yes. PDF and DOCX exports are available on paid plans. Business and Enterprise plans include white-label reports with your own logo, colours, and contact details.

q4.question: How does continuous monitoring work?
q4.answer: Schedule automatic scans daily, weekly, or monthly. VexNexa alerts you via email when scores drop or new critical issues appear. Track trends over time from your dashboard.

q5.question: Is VexNexa an accessibility overlay?
q5.answer: No. We scan your code structure and report real WCAG violations. We never inject widgets or scripts into your site. Our reports help developers fix issues at the source.

assurance.title: Accessibility Assurance
assurance.subtitle: Continuous monitoring and compliance
assurance.description: Stay compliant with automated monitoring and alerts

hero.title: Run a WCAG Scan.
hero.subtitle: Paste a URL, hit scan, and see every WCAG violation ranked by severity — with the affected elements and fix guidance you actually need.
hero.note: No credit card required. Most single-page scans complete in under a minute.

problem.title: Most WCAG Scans Give You a Number. Not a Plan.
problem.subtitle: You've probably tried a free accessibility checker before. It spit out a score, listed some errors, and left you staring at cryptic rule IDs wondering what to fix first.

flatErrors.title: Flat error lists
flatErrors.description: No priority ranking — a missing alt tag is listed next to a keyboard trap.

noElementContext.title: No element context
noElementContext.description: You know a rule failed, but not which element on which page section.

staleResults.title: Stale results
staleResults.description: One-off scans with no way to track whether last month's fixes actually stuck.

solution.title: A WCAG Scan That Actually Tells You What to Do
solution.subtitle: VexNexa scans against WCAG 2.1 AA and 2.2 criteria using the axe-core engine — the same engine trusted by Microsoft, Google, and the U.S. government.

severityRanked.title: Severity-Ranked Violations
severityRanked.description: Every issue is scored by impact — critical, serious, moderate, or minor. Fix what matters first.

elementDetail.title: Element-Level Detail
elementDetail.description: See the exact CSS selector, HTML snippet, and page location for each violation. No guesswork.

healthScore.title: Health Score (0–100)
healthScore.description: A weighted score that accounts for issue count and severity. Track improvement over time.

exportReports.title: Export-Ready Reports
exportReports.description: Download your WCAG scan results as PDF or Word with full violation details and fix guidance.

howItWorks.title: Four Steps from URL to Fix List

enterUrl.title: Enter a URL
enterUrl.description: Paste any page URL into the scanner. Public or authenticated — VexNexa handles both.

scanRuns.title: Scan runs in seconds
scanRuns.description: The axe-core engine evaluates the page against WCAG 2.1 AA and selected 2.2 criteria in real-time.

reviewResults.title: Review ranked results
reviewResults.description: Violations appear sorted by severity with affected elements, fix guidance, and WCAG criterion references.

exportShare.title: Export or share
exportShare.description: Download a PDF or DOCX report. Share results with your team or attach to a client deliverable.

comparison.title: VexNexa vs. Basic Checkers
comparison.subtitle: Free checkers are fine for a quick glance. When you need to prioritize, report, and track — you need more.

useCases.title: Built for Teams That Ship Accessible Products

siteOwners.title: Site Owners & Marketing Teams
siteOwners.description: Verify your public pages meet WCAG AA before a redesign launch or regulatory deadline.

developers.title: Developers & QA Engineers
developers.description: Integrate WCAG scans into your sprint workflow. Catch regressions before they reach production.

compliance.title: Compliance & Legal Teams
compliance.description: Generate structured reports to document your accessibility posture for audits and procurement.

finalCTA.title: Ready to Scan Your Site?
finalCTA.subtitle: Get actionable WCAG violation reports in minutes. No credit card required for your first scan.

complianceReports.title: Compliance Reports
complianceReports.description: Turn scan results into executive-ready PDF and DOCX reports.

accessibilityChecker.title: Accessibility Checker
accessibilityChecker.description: Compare VexNexa to basic checkers and see what you've been missing.

ongoingMonitoring.title: Ongoing Monitoring
ongoingMonitoring.description: Schedule recurring scans and track accessibility trends over time.

hero.title: WCAG Compliance Reports
hero.subtitle: Raw scan data doesn't get budget approved. VexNexa generates structured compliance reports with executive summaries, severity breakdowns, and prioritized fix lists — ready to attach to audits, tenders, and board presentations.
hero.note: Free trial. No credit card required. First report in minutes.

reportingGap.title: Scan Results Alone Don't Convince Decision-Makers
reportingGap.subtitle: You know the site has accessibility issues. The scanner confirmed it. But forwarding a screenshot of a tool's output to your compliance officer, procurement lead, or executive sponsor rarely moves the needle.
reportingGap.context: Decision-makers need context: how bad is it, what should be fixed first, what does remediation look like, and how does the current state map to WCAG criteria. That requires a report — not a dashboard.

reportStructure.title: Inside a VexNexa Compliance Report
reportStructure.subtitle: Every section is structured for a specific reader — from executives who need the one-page summary to developers who need the element selectors.

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.description: Health score, grade (A through F), risk level, and a plain-language overview. Designed for non-technical readers who need the headline, not the code.

severityBreakdown.title: Severity Breakdown
severityBreakdown.description: Issue counts by severity tier — critical, serious, moderate, minor. Visual donut chart and progress bars make the distribution immediately clear.

wcagMatrix.title: WCAG Compliance Matrix
wcagMatrix.description: A criterion-by-criterion table showing pass, fail, needs manual review, or not tested for each relevant WCAG success criterion.

topPriority.title: Top Priority Fixes
topPriority.description: The highest-impact issues ranked by weighted severity. Each entry includes the affected element count and an impact score so teams know where to start.

detailedList.title: Detailed Issue List
detailedList.description: Every violation with its WCAG criterion, severity, affected CSS selectors, HTML snippets, and a link to the relevant WCAG understanding document.

eaaReadiness.title: EAA Readiness Indicator
eaaReadiness.description: For European compliance workflows: an EAA 2025 readiness badge and notes on how the site maps to European Accessibility Act requirements.

pdf.title: PDF Report
pdf.description: Print-optimized layout with visual charts, branded cover page, and professional typography. Ideal for sharing with stakeholders, attaching to RFP responses, or printing for in-person meetings.

features.paginated: Paginated with running headers and footers
features.charts: Donut charts, progress bars, and score cards
features.print: Direct browser print — no conversion needed

docx.title: DOCX Report
docx.description: Editable Word document with the same content structure. Add your own commentary, customize for specific clients, or paste into existing proposal templates.

features.editable: Fully editable in Microsoft Word or Google Docs
features.formatting: Tables, styled text, and severity indicators

audience.title: Reports Designed for the People Who Make Decisions

compliance.title: Compliance Officers
compliance.useCase: Attach WCAG compliance documentation to internal audits and regulatory filings. The WCAG matrix provides criterion-level evidence.

agency.title: Agency Teams
agency.useCase: Deliver professional reports to clients under your own branding. Show progress over time and justify accessibility budgets.

procurement.title: Procurement & Legal
procurement.useCase: Evaluate vendor accessibility claims with structured evidence. Use reports as part of tender requirements and contract compliance.

scope.title: A Compliance Report, Not a Compliance Guarantee
scope.description: VexNexa reports document what automated testing detects. They cover a meaningful range of WCAG success criteria, but full conformance requires both automated and manual assessment. The report clearly marks which criteria were tested, which passed, and which need manual review.
scope.disclaimer: Use VexNexa reports as part of a broader compliance process — not as a standalone legal certification.

q1.question: What WCAG criteria does the compliance report cover?
q1.answer: The report covers testable WCAG 2.1 Level AA criteria and selected WCAG 2.2 criteria. The WCAG compliance matrix explicitly shows each criterion's status — pass, fail, needs manual review, or not tested — so there's no ambiguity about coverage.

q2.question: Can I customize the report before sharing it?
q2.answer: Yes. The DOCX export is fully editable — you can add commentary, remove sections, or paste content into your own templates. The PDF format is finalized for direct sharing or printing.

q3.question: Does the report include remediation guidance?
q3.answer: Each violation includes a description of the issue, why it matters, and a link to the relevant WCAG understanding document. The top priority fixes section ranks issues by impact to help teams plan their remediation sprints.

q4.question: How is this different from a basic accessibility checker output?
q4.answer: Basic checkers give you raw data — a list of violations and maybe a score. VexNexa compliance reports structure that data for decision-makers: executive summaries, severity breakdowns, WCAG matrices, and prioritized action lists. The report is designed to move projects forward, not just identify problems.

q5.question: Can I use these reports for legal compliance?
q5.answer: VexNexa reports provide strong evidence of accessibility testing and remediation planning. However, they should be used as part of a broader compliance strategy that includes manual testing and expert review. The reports clearly indicate which criteria were tested automatically versus which require manual assessment.

q6.question: Do reports include white-label branding?
q6.answer: Yes. Business and Enterprise plans include full white-label branding with your company logo, colors, and contact information. The reports appear as if generated by your own team, with no VexNexa branding visible.

finalCTA.title: Generate a Report That Gets Read
finalCTA.subtitle: Scan a URL, review the findings, and download a structured WCAG compliance report — all in under five minutes.

hero.title: An Accessibility Checker That
hero.subtitle: Stop scrolling through unsorted error dumps. VexNexa checks your site for WCAG violations and shows you exactly which issues are critical, which are minor, and where each one lives in your HTML.
hero.note: Free check. No credit card. Results in seconds.

passFailProblem.title: Why "78% Accessible" Tells You Almost Nothing
passFailProblem.subtitle: A single percentage hides more than it reveals. Two sites can both score 78% — one has three critical keyboard traps, the other has forty missing alt texts on decorative images. The first is unusable. The second is mostly fine.
passFailProblem.solution: Basic accessibility checkers treat every failed rule equally. VexNexa doesn't. Every violation is weighted by real-world impact so you can separate the urgent from the cosmetic.

whatYouGet.title: What a Real Accessibility Check Looks Like
whatYouGet.subtitle: VexNexa goes beyond simple pass/fail testing. Get actionable insights that help you prioritize fixes and track improvement over time.

weightedScore.title: Weighted Health Score
weightedScore.description: Not a simple ratio. Critical issues weigh more than minor ones, giving you a score that reflects actual user impact.

severityTiers.title: Severity Tiers
severityTiers.description: Four tiers — Critical, Serious, Moderate, Minor — so your team can triage without guessing. Critical issues always appear first.

elementDetail.title: Element-Level Detail
elementDetail.description: See the exact CSS selector and HTML snippet for each violation. No hunting through your code to find the problem.

wcagReferences.title: WCAG Criterion References
wcagReferences.description: Every violation links to the specific WCAG success criterion, with plain-language explanations of why it matters.

riskAssessment.title: Risk Assessment
riskAssessment.description: Understand which issues block users completely versus which are minor inconveniences. Prioritize fixes that actually improve accessibility.

trendTracking.title: Trend Tracking
trendTracking.description: Monitor how your score changes over time. See if new features improve accessibility or if regressions are creeping in.

comparison.title: How VexNexa Compares to Free Checkers
comparison.subtitle: Free accessibility checkers are useful for a quick glance. But when you need to prioritize fixes, document compliance, or track improvement, you need more than a simple pass/fail result.

freelancers.title: Freelancers & Consultants

limitations.title: What Automated Checking Can't Do
limitations.description: No tool catches everything. Automated accessibility checkers reliably detect issues like missing alt text, color contrast failures, and missing form labels. But criteria that depend on content meaning, logical reading order, or complex keyboard interactions still need a human reviewer.
limitations.solution: VexNexa flags where manual review is recommended and helps you prioritize what automated testing does find — so your team spends expert time where it counts.

q1.question: How is VexNexa different from WAVE or Lighthouse?
q1.answer: WAVE and Lighthouse are excellent free tools for quick spot-checks. VexNexa adds severity ranking, element-level CSS selectors, exportable PDF/DOCX reports, score tracking over time, and white-label branding — features designed for teams that need to act on results, not just view them.

q2.question: Can I check more than one page at a time?
q2.answer: Yes. Paid plans support multi-page scans across your site. Each page produces its own severity-ranked violation list and contributes to an overall site health score.

q3.question: What accessibility standards does VexNexa check against?
q3.answer: VexNexa checks against WCAG 2.1 Level AA and selected WCAG 2.2 criteria using the axe-core engine. Each reported violation references the specific success criterion it relates to.

q4.question: Do I need to create an account?
q4.answer: You can run a single-page scan without an account to see the results. For ongoing monitoring, multiple-page scans, or to export reports, you'll need to create a free account.

q5.question: How accurate are the results?
q5.answer: VexNexa uses the axe-core engine — the same trusted engine used by Microsoft, Google, and the U.S. government. It reliably detects testable issues with high precision. However, automated testing can't catch everything — that's why VexNexa flags where manual review is recommended.
